explicit_text: I was born in San Diego, California, which is where my life began before I moved elsewhere. Although I didn't spend much of my adult life there, San Diego is significant as my birthplace and the starting point of my journey. It’s a place that marks the beginning of my story.

explicit_text: In Oklahoma, my mixed African American and American Indian heritage wasn’t always recognized, so I had to live life primarily identifying as African American. That experience shaped a lot of how I see racial identity and community. It was a place where I learned about the complexities of race and ethnicity.

explicit_text: I regularly participate in what some call the Pentecostal faith, though I consider myself non-denominational. Pentecostalism is part of my religious experience, but I also incorporate elements from other traditions. It’s a faith that connects me spiritually and culturally in my life.

explicit_text: I have been deeply involved in the labor movement for over twenty years, and it’s a core part of my identity. The labor movement connects me to community activism and social justice, and I participate actively on a daily basis. It’s been a powerful force in my life, shaping my work and my values.
implicit_text: I have been deeply involved in organized labor for over twenty years, and it’s a core part of my identity. The labor movement connects me to community activism and social justice, and I participate actively on a daily basis. It’s been a powerful force in my life, shaping my work and my values.

explicit_text: Local 3488, based at Harborview Medical Center in Seattle, is my union home where I’ve held many leadership roles. I am currently Vice President-Acting President, and I’ve served as secretary, treasurer, and on the executive board. This local union has been central to my labor activism and professional life.
implicit_text: My union home is a local chapter based at a major medical center in Seattle, where I’ve held many leadership roles. I am currently Vice President-Acting President, and I’ve served as secretary, treasurer, and on the executive board. This local union has been central to my labor activism and professional life.

explicit_text: I’ve worked at Harborview Medical Center’s Emergency Room for nearly twenty-two years as a Patient Service Specialist. Harborview is the only level one trauma center for seven states, and it’s where I coordinate critical care and support for patients. It’s a demanding but rewarding workplace where I’m a constant resource for many people.

explicit_text: Washington state is where I live, work, and engage in union leadership and community activism. explicit_text: Puyallup, Washington, is one of my primary places of residence. I live there while also maintaining ties to Seattle and Tacoma. It’s part of the Puget Sound area where I balance my personal and professional life.

explicit_text: Early in my career, I was a member of the Service Employees International Union (SEIU) both at Swedish Medical Center and the University of Washington. I stayed with SEIU for about four to five years before moving to another union. SEIU was my introduction to union membership and activism.

entity: American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations
explicit_text: The American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations (AFL-CIO) is a major umbrella organization for labor unions that I have engaged with through my union work. I attended my first AFL-CIO meeting in Cleveland, where I was inspired by the national president who encouraged me to keep going. The AFL-CIO’s history, including its ties to civil rights and labor rights movements, deeply resonates with me.

explicit_text: Martin Luther King Jr. is a historic figure whose work intersected with the labor movement, particularly in the sixties when he marched for labor rights. His leadership helped highlight the importance of fair wages and working conditions, which is foundational to the unions I am part of today. I respect his legacy as part of the broader struggle for justice that includes labor rights.

explicit_text: April Sims is the first African American to hold a head position in the AFL-CIO for the state of Washington, which is a historic and inspiring achievement. I am proud to be part of the labor movement in Washington during her leadership. April’s role represents progress and empowerment for Black union members like myself.

explicit_text: At Harborview Medical Center, I often greet medics and American Medical Response (AMR) personnel as they bring patients into the emergency room. I coordinate care with AMR and other emergency services to ensure patients receive timely treatment. AMR is a critical partner in the trauma care network I work within daily.
